headline: RARE EARTH INDEPENDENCE: Should You Buy USA Rare Earth (USAR) While It's Below $20?
snippet: USA Rare Earth sits ~45% below its 52-week high despite $3.1B in combined U.S. government and private backing, a commissioned Phase 1a sintered magnet line targeting 600 mt/yr by end-2026, and an average analyst price target implying ~70% upside. The Motley Fool examines whether the pullback is an entry point or a warning sign.

headline: $7,000 GOLD INCOMING? How Junior Mining Stocks Could Deliver 20 Baggers — Don Durrett
snippet: Mining analyst Don Durrett argues gold is tracking toward $7,000/oz and that under-owned junior producers could deliver 20x returns as institutional money rotates from bullion ETFs into equities. He points to record miner margins, compressed valuations, and central bank buying averaging 585 tonnes per quarter as the setup most retail investors are still ignoring.

headline: THE RESERVE CRISIS: Major Miners Can't Replace What They're Digging Up — Juniors Hold the Answer
snippet: A new analysis argues that reserve depletion at the major mining houses is accelerating faster than internal exploration budgets can offset, forcing seniors to look to junior acquisitions for resource replenishment. With gold reserve replacement ratios falling below 1:1 at several producers, the piece makes the case that discovery-stage juniors are positioned as strategic acquisition targets.

headline: Q-GOLD SURGES 103%: Maiden PEA for Quartz Mountain Tops This Week's TSX-V Gainers
snippet: Q-Gold Resources led TSX-V gainers this week with shares more than doubling after releasing a maiden preliminary economic assessment for its Quartz Mountain project. The move underscores how quickly a credible scoping study can reprice a junior in the current gold bull market, with investors rewarding any project that demonstrates viable economics.

headline: 103 METRES OF GOLD: KLDC's First Drill Hole at Mirado Hits 5.66 g/t Over 18.2m Near Kirkland Lake
snippet: Kirkland Lake Discoveries (TSXV: KLDC) reported its first 2026 drill hole at the past-producing Mirado property — 20 km southeast of Kirkland Lake, Ontario — returned 103 metres of continuous gold mineralization, including 5.66 g/t Au over 18.2m and 3.30 g/t Au over 18.7m. The company used Chrysos PhotonAssay technology for faster, more precise assays across larger sample sizes.

headline: DISCOVERY PREMIUM: What Wall Street Actually Pays When Copper and Gold Overlap on One Property
snippet: An analysis examines the valuation premium awarded to junior explorers whose drill programs intersect copper-gold porphyry systems, finding that discovery holes combining both metals can command multiples well above single-metal peers. With copper near $6.10/lb and gold above $4,800/oz, the combined economics have never been more compelling for district-scale copper-gold explorers.

headline: CONTACT LAKE DELIVERS: Trident Hits 4.61 g/t Gold Over 38.5m, Plus 11.97 g/t Over 10.7m at Depth
snippet: Trident Resources reported assay results from the 2026 winter program at the Contact Lake Gold Project in northern Saskatchewan, with hole CL26028 intersecting 4.61 g/t Au over 38.48m from 77m depth — 200m northeast of 2025 drilling — expanding the known footprint of the Bakos Shear zone. A second hole, CL26025, cut 11.97 g/t Au over 10.70m from 324m, confirming depth continuity.

headline: ENERGY FUELS TRANSITIONS: Ross Bhappu Takes Over as CEO, Signals Next Growth Phase for Uranium-REE Giant
snippet: Energy Fuels (NYSE: UUUU, TSX: EFR), the largest U.S. uranium producer and an emerging rare earth elements leader, appointed Ross Bhappu as CEO effective April 15, succeeding Mark Chalmers who is retiring after eight years. Bhappu, who has served as President since August 2025, brings three decades of mining and capital markets experience and will oversee the company's integrated uranium-to-rare-earth expansion.

headline: SILVER AT DECADE HIGHS: Metal Surges 5% as Hormuz Ceasefire Eases Industrial Demand Fears
snippet: Silver climbed ~5% toward $82/oz after the U.S. and Iran confirmed the Strait of Hormuz would remain fully open during a 10-day ceasefire, reducing supply-chain disruption fears for industrial users. Silver has risen more than 150% over the past year, reaching decade highs driven by solar, EV, and electronics demand that structural bulls argue is only beginning.
